message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>Viruses such as SARS-CoV-2 infect the human body by forming interactions between virus proteins and human proteins. However, experimental methods to find protein interactions are inadequate: large scale experiments are noisy, and small scale experiments are slow and expensive. Inspired by the recent successes of deep neural networks, we hypothesize that deep learning methods are well-positioned to aid and augment biological experiments, hoping to help identify more accurate virus-host protein interaction maps. Moreover, computational methods can quickly adapt to predict how virus mutations change protein interactions with the host proteins.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>We propose DeepVHPPI, a novel deep learning framework combining a self-attention-based transformer architecture and a transfer learning training strategy to predict interactions between human proteins and virus proteins that have novel sequence patterns. We show that our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art methods significantly in predicting Virus–Human protein interactions for SARS-CoV-2, H1N1, and Ebola. In addition, we demonstrate how our framework can be used to predict and interpret the interactions of mutated SARS-CoV-2 Spike protein sequences.</jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Availability</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We make all of our data and code available on GitHub
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://github.com/QData/DeepVHPPI">https://github.com/QData/DeepVHPPI</jats:ext-link>
                    .
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>ACM Reference Format</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Jack Lanchantin, Tom Weingarten, Arshdeep Sekhon, Clint Miller, and Yanjun Qi. 2021. Transfer Learning for Predicting Virus-Host Protein Interactions for Novel Virus Sequences. In
                    <jats:italic>Proceedings of ACM Conference (ACM-BCB)</jats:italic>
                    . ACM, New York, NY, USA, 10 pages. https://doi.org/??
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Transfer Learning for Predicting Virus-Host Protein Interactions for Novel Virus Sequences
